stories.headline: Georgia Senate GOP primary heads to runoff where electability is a top issue
stories.summary: Georgia was among six states where voters participated in primaries Tuesday. Its Senate race is among a handful that could determine party control next year.

stories.headline: Global Long Bond Yields Hit Highest in Almost Two Decades
stories.summary: The selloff in longer-maturity government bonds has pushed up yields to levels last seen during the global financial crisis, and market participants are warning the move has room to run.

stories.headline: Thomas Massie Loses Kentucky House Primary to Trump-Backed Ed Gallrein
stories.summary: Representative Thomas Massie, President Trump’s chief G.O.P. antagonist in the House, lost to Ed Gallrein, Mr. Trump’s handpicked candidate.

stories.headline: Blinded and broken, Sunny the owl becomes another casualty of Russia’s war
stories.summary: Ukrainians lament appalling toll of fighting  on their country’s bird populationRussia sent kamikaze drones to attack the Ukrainian city of Zaporizhzhia in February. They hit buildings and killed several people. One unreported victim of the bombardment was a male long-eared owl, blinded in one eye and found with a badly broken wing. A passerby scooped up the stunned bird, put him in a box and took him to the city of Dnipro.The owl – nicknamed Sun

stories.headline: Iran war day 82: Tehran warns of ‘new fronts’ as Trump sets deadline
stories.summary: Iran says it learned from past clashes as Trump gives Tehran 'two to three days' to reach a deal.

stories.headline: UN cuts global growth forecast, blaming Middle East crisis

stories.headline: Republican Thomas Massie who stood up to Trump defeated in Kentucky primary
stories.summary: With an estimated 72 percent of the vote counted, Ed Gallrein led with 54.4 percent to Massie’s 45.6 percent.

stories.headline: After over a year of tit-for-tat, the US and China could slash sky-high tariffs
stories.summary: After more than a year of escalating trade tensions, Beijing and Washington say they are ready to cut tariffs on tens of billions of dollars worth of goods — but analysts warn the impact on growth may be limited.

stories.headline: Von der Leyen calls for completing  the European single market in EU parliament address
stories.summary: Commission chief Ursula von der Leyen called for completing the European single market by cutting red tape, boosting the digital transition, and maintaining social inclusion. But she also warned that success still requires constant political will.

stories.headline: ‘We have other countermeasures ready’ if US disrespects EU trade deal, says top negotiator Lange
stories.summary: EU lawmakers approved the controversial Turnberry Agreement after Donald Trump threatened steep tariffs on European cars. EU trade chief Bernd Lange called it Europe’s best shield against Trump-era trade shocks, while warning the bloc remains vulnerable to sudden White House moves.

stories.headline: Trump exerts iron grip on Republican Party with Massie defeated
stories.summary: Victory over a Republican rebel in Kentucky shows the president's strength but his power comes with risks for the midterms.

stories.headline: Lithuania's leaders take shelter during drone air alert

stories.headline: Trump endorses Paxton in Texas, gambling on a challenger with baggage in a crucial race
stories.summary: Trump's move dismayed Senate Republicans, many of whom have served with the Texan for decades.

stories.headline: Lebanon says 21 killed, including children, in Israeli air strikes
stories.summary: Twelve people were killed in a single strike on a house, including three children and three women, Lebanon's state-run news agency says.

stories.headline: Senate Primary in Alabama to Replace Tommy Tuberville Heads to Runoff

stories.headline: Mike Collins and Derek Dooley Head to G.O.P. Senate Runoff in Georgia
stories.summary: Mr. Collins and Mr. Dooley are set to face each other on June 16 as they compete for the chance to challenge Senator Jon Ossoff, a popular Democrat.

stories.headline: Republican Primary for Georgia Governor Will Go to a Runoff
stories.summary: The state’s lieutenant governor, Burt Jones, will face a billionaire health care executive, Rick Jackson, on June 16 to decide the party’s standard-bearer to succeed Gov. Brian Kemp.

stories.headline: Vladimir Putin arrives in Beijing for state visit hot on heels of Trump
stories.summary: Russian president is welcomed to Beijing with an honour guard after saying relations with China have reached an ‘unprecedented level’  Vladimir Putin has arrived in Beijing for a state visit, four days after Donald Trump left China.Putin was greeted by China’s foreign minister, Wang Yi, when he landed on Tuesday evening, with an honour guard alongside Chinese youths waving China and Russia’s national flags in a welcome ceremony on the tarmac. stories.headline: Melbourne teenager Bianca Adler becomes youngest Australian to climb Mount Everest
stories.summary: The 18-year-old high school student reached the top of the world’s tallest mountain on her second attemptAn 18-year-old high school student from Melbourne became the youngest Australian to climb to the top of Mount Everest on Wednesday.According to her Garmin data and a post on Instagram, Bianca Adler reached the 8,849 metre summit at nearly 6.30am Melbourne time, and nearly 2.30am Nepal time, with her guides, Pemba and Ngdu. stories.headline: Emerging Stocks at Two-Week Low, Currencies Stay Under Pressure
stories.summary: Currencies in India and Indonesia edged off record lows after central banks stepped up efforts to stem the losses, while swaps priced a South African interest-rate hike, reflecting the continued heavy pressure on emerging markets.

stories.summary: India’s treasury bill yields surged at a weekly auction, signaling fixed-income traders are starting to price in potential interest-rate hikes by the central bank.

stories.headline: UBS Chief Strategist Sees US Consumer Slowdown Threatening Stock Rally
stories.summary: The US consumer will slow down as real disposable income growth nears zero and fiscal support fades, threatening equity markets despite strong first-quarter earnings driven by artificial intelligence, according to Bhanu Baweja, chief strategist at UBS Group AG.

stories.headline: US Mortgage Rates Rose to Almost Two-Month High in Latest Week

stories.headline: Nickel Trims Gains With Indonesia Export Controls in Focus

stories.headline: France’s Moulin Backs Shared Euro Debt for New Projects

stories.headline: Singapore Tops Indonesia as Biggest Southeast Asia Stock Market
stories.summary: Singapore has overtaken Indonesia as Southeast Asia’s largest stock market, signaling investors’ endorsement of the city-state’s market reforms and their diminishing confidence in the larger country’s economic management.

stories.headline: Indonesia’s Radical Export Experiment Upends Its Commodity Trade
stories.summary: Indonesia’s radical plan to take control of key commodity exports has left the country’s coal miners, palm-oil producers and traders racing to understand details of a policy that throws into upheaval one of the country’s biggest industries.

stories.headline: Bob Brooks, Labor Leader Backed by Top Democrats, Wins Pennsylvania House Primary
stories.summary: Mr. Brooks will face Representative Ryan Mackenzie, a Republican, in the fall. He earned endorsements from across his party, including Senator Bernie Sanders of Vermont and Gov. Josh Shapiro of Pennsylvania.

stories.headline: A Tuberville-Jones Rematch Is Set for the Alabama Governor Race
stories.summary: Senator Tommy Tuberville, a Republican, will face the Democrat that he defeated in 2020, Doug Jones, after both easily won their primaries.

stories.headline: Israeli minister Smotrich says ICC prosecutor seeking warrant for his arrest
